articles.title: CRYPTO FUNDS SEE $2B WEEKLY OUTFLOWS
articles.summary: CoinShares reported $2 billion in outflows from global crypto investment products, the largest weekly exit since February, as shifting US rate cut expectations and selling from large holders dampened sentiment. The US led with 97% of redemptions, while Germany bucked the trend with $13 million in inflows. Bitcoin funds saw $1.38 billion in withdrawals and Ethereum $689 million, while multi-asset ETPs attracted $69 million as investors sought diversified exposure amid macro-driven volatility.

articles.title: BITCOIN'S 25% SLIDE REFLECTS SHORT-TERM CORRECTION
articles.summary: Bitcoin's 25% drop from its October 6 all-time high of $126,000 reflects investor anxiety over historical four-year cycle peaks rather than weakening fundamentals. 340,000 BTC sold by long-term holders was absorbed by $34 billion in ETF inflows and corporate treasuries. Institutional ETF ownership climbed from 20% to 28% with $125 billion in assets under management despite recent outflows, while Strategy's conservative leverage ratio of $8 billion debt against $61 billion in bitcoin holdings dismisses liquidation concerns. A shallow correction is expected rather than the typical 60-70% drawdown as structural tailwinds remain intact.

articles.title: 1INCH LAUNCHES ‘AQUA' TO UNIFY DEFI LIQUIDITY
articles.summary: 1inch Aqua is a shared liquidity model designed to eliminate DeFi's capital fragmentation by turning every wallet into a self-custodial AMM, allowing the same capital to power multiple strategies simultaneously. Developers can now access Aqua's SDK, libraries, and bounties up to $100,000. Aqua is positioned as a foundational liquidity primitive that removes deposit and withdrawal logic while boosting efficiency across chains. The launch at Devconnect builds on 1inch's aggregation success and aims to create a capital-efficient base layer for DeFi composability.

articles.title: AAVE APP: A SMARTER WAY TO SAVE
articles.summary: Aave is launching an app that allows users to earn up to 9% on stablecoin balances. It uses embedded wallets to create an email-based sign-up solution, mitigating the need to interact with a complex browser wallet and web interface. Aave will offer insurance on deposits up to $1 million per user. The waitlist is currently live. There is no clear launch date yet.

articles.title: HOW YIELD IS ACTUALLY MADE
articles.summary: DeFi vaults automate yield generation through smart contracts that deposit assets, allocate to protocols like Aave and Curve, compound returns, and continuously rebalance positions across nine core strategies, including yield aggregation, delta-neutral hedging, leveraged farming, and options-based derivatives. The Stream Finance case exposed systemic risk when curator failures triggered $93 million in cascading losses across interconnected protocols, demonstrating how recursive vault loops and poor management amplifies contagion beyond protocol boundaries. Vaults simplified DeFi from complex multi-step strategies into single-action deposits, but the next phase requires stricter audits, DAO-based curator approvals, and transparent proof-of-reserves dashboards to balance automation with accountability as smart contract bugs and oracle manipulation remain persistent attack vectors.

articles.title: WHY YOUR COIN ISN'T PUMPING
articles.summary: Crypto prices remain depressed despite ETF launches and institutional adoption because blockchains trade at software multiples on casino revenue, with Ethereum at 200-400x price-to-sales on $1-2 billion in cyclical fees versus Nvidia's 40-45x price-to-earnings with recurring contracts. The industry deployed over $100 billion into infrastructure, while the entire crypto ecosystem serves roughly 40 million active users, compared to OpenAI's rumored $1 trillion IPO valuation serving 20x more users. Ethereum faces structural value leakage as L2s capture fees while the base layer carries security costs, creating a federal government collecting only state-level tax revenues, with most blockchain revenue tied to perpetuals, memecoins, and MEV spikes rather than stable enterprise cash flows.

articles.title: ETHEREUM'S ECONOMIC AND PRIVACY EDGE OVER BITCOIN
articles.summary: Ethereum's rollup-centric architecture offers privacy without sacrificing composability, unlike Bitcoin or Zcash, allowing private interaction with DeFi and financial apps. ETH is also a superior store of value due to its native staking yield, which stays above net issuance, eliminating miner-driven sell pressure. Ethereum-based Digital Asset Treasuries (DATs) are therefore structured around ETH as both a yield-generating and privacy-preserving asset.

articles.title: REVISITING THE HYPEREVM THESIS
articles.summary: Three months after predicting HyperEVM would become crypto's most aligned L1, the thesis faces market pressure as TVL dropped $500 million and HYPE fell 13.5% to $38.50 amid industry-wide airdrop farmer exhaustion following October's Black Friday crash. Native Markets launched USDH, the first stablecoin using 50% of yield for HYPE buybacks, while HIP-3 projects like Kinetiq and Ventuals deployed leveraged equities and pre-IPO markets but struggle with liquidity despite Robinhood listings and expanding builder code distribution. The approaching one-year HYPE TGE anniversary brings team unlock concerns, yet HyperEVM remains crypto's only chain where L1 token, infrastructure, and builders are economically aligned through mechanisms impossible elsewhere, like delta-neutral LP strategies combining AMMs with native perp DEX primitives.

articles.title: ARTEMIS RELEASES 2025 STABLECOIN ECOSYSTEM MAP
articles.summary: Artemis's 2025 Stablecoin Map visualizes over 250 projects across the stablecoin landscape from issuers like Circle and PayPal to infrastructure players like Iron and BlockRadar.

articles.title: EIP-7918 TO FIX ETHEREUM'S BLOB PRICING FLAW
articles.summary: Ethereum's current blob pricing misreads reduced blob usage during periods of high L1 gas as low demand, making blobs artificially cheap and causing L2s to halt postings.

articles.title: POLYGON LEADS P2P PAYMENTS WITH 40% MARKET SHARE
THE COMPETITIVE MOAT OF ONCHAIN FIRMS
articles.summary: In the onchain economy, firms extracting value for insiders will lose to hyper-efficient onchain organizations that circulate surplus directly to users.
